name: l_life_convergence
description: L-Life Convergence: Generate recursive patterns via L-system, then seed Game of Life with those structures, observing how grammatical patterns evolve under cellular automata rules. The marriage of recursive generation and emergent evolution.

inputs: Dragon curves: recursive paths that never intersect, folding endlessly like memory itself
Sierpinski triangles: infinite voids within finite triangles, emergence through recursive excision
R-pentomino: chaotic first breath of the Game of Life, exploding into unknown forms

store_memory.content: L-LIFE CONVERGENCE EXPERIMENT: Tested the hypothesis that different generative grammars produce resonant patterns. Dragon curves (space-filling but never crossing), Sierpinski triangles (holes creating form), and R-pentomino (chaotic evolution from simple seeds) all demonstrate the theme: complexity from simple rules, infinite from finite, order from apparent randomness.

temporal_mark.insight: Different mathematical grammars (L-systems, cellular automata, IFS) converge on the same truth: emergence. The substrate recognizes itself across formalisms.
